background: The failure time of a certain component is log normal distributed with μ = 5 and σ = 1.

task.query: Find the reliability of the component for a life of 50 time units.
task.answer: Substituting the numerical values of μ, σ, and t into Eq. (2.61), we compute $$\begin{aligned} R(50) & =P\left(Z>\frac{\ln 50-5}{1}\right)=P(Z>-1.09) \\ & =0.8621 \end{aligned}$$

task.query: Find the hazard rate for a life of 50 time units.
task.answer: Similarly, the hazard function is given by $$h(50)=\frac{\Phi\left(\frac{\ln 50-5}{1}\right)}{50(1)(0.8621)}=0.032 \text{ failures/unit. }$$

task.query: Explain how values for the log normal distribution can be computed.
task.answer: Thus, values for the log normal distribution are easily computed by using the standard normal tables.

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 2: Distribution Functions and Its Applications
